SEARCH
Package org.finos.fluxnova.bpm.engine.impl.bpmn.behavior

Class AdHocSubProcessActivityBehavior

java.lang.Object
org.finos.fluxnova.bpm.engine.impl.bpmn.behavior.FlowNodeActivityBehavior
org.finos.fluxnova.bpm.engine.impl.bpmn.behavior.AbstractBpmnActivityBehavior
org.finos.fluxnova.bpm.engine.impl.bpmn.behavior.AdHocSubProcessActivityBehavior
All Implemented Interfaces:
CoreActivityBehavior<ActivityExecution>, ActivityBehavior, AdHocCompositeActivityBehavior, CompositeActivityBehavior, SignallableActivityBehavior
public class AdHocSubProcessActivityBehavior extends AbstractBpmnActivityBehavior implements AdHocCompositeActivityBehavior
Implementation of the BPMN 2.0 Ad-Hoc Sub-Process.

An Ad-Hoc Sub-Process is a specialized type of Sub-Process that has a set of Activities that can be performed in any order, and some of which may not be performed at all. Initial activities are activated from the activeTasksCollection extension property and additional starter activities may be activated via RuntimeService#triggerAdHocActivities(String, Collection, Map).

The subprocess completes when the completionCondition evaluates to true after any inner activity completes. If no completion condition is defined, default auto-complete semantics apply: once at least one ad-hoc activity has been started and no child activities remain active, the scope is completed. This behavior can be disabled by setting extension attribute autoComplete to false, in which case explicit completion is required.

  • Method Details

    • execute

      public void execute(ActivityExecution execution) throws Exception
      On entry into an ad-hoc subprocess, only starter activities named in the optional activeTasksCollection extension property are activated in parallel.
      Specified by:
      execute in interface ActivityBehavior
      Specified by:
      execute in interface CoreActivityBehavior<ActivityExecution>
      Overrides:
      execute in class FlowNodeActivityBehavior
      Throws:
      Exception

    • concurrentChildExecutionEnded

      public void concurrentChildExecutionEnded(ActivityExecution scopeExecution, ActivityExecution endedExecution)
      Called by the PVM each time a concurrent child execution within this ad-hoc scope completes. Removes the ended execution, re-evaluates the completion condition and — if met — cancels any remaining running activities and leaves the subprocess.
      Specified by:
      concurrentChildExecutionEnded in interface CompositeActivityBehavior
      Parameters:
      scopeExecution - scope execution for the activity which defined the behavior
      endedExecution - the execution which ended

    • complete

      public void complete(ActivityExecution scopeExecution)
      Called by the PVM when all concurrent executions inside the scope have finished. For ad-hoc, this is the last chance to evaluate the completion condition. If the condition is met (or not defined) the subprocess proceeds; otherwise the scope execution stays open for further triggers.
      Specified by:
      complete in interface CompositeActivityBehavior

    • evaluateCompletionCondition

      protected void evaluateCompletionCondition(ActivityExecution scopeExecution)
      Evaluates the optional completion condition. Exits the subprocess if the condition is true or if no condition is configured. If the condition is present but evaluates to false the scope execution simply remains open.

    • evaluateCompletionCondition

      protected void evaluateCompletionCondition(ActivityExecution scopeExecution, ActivityImpl scopeActivity)

    • shouldCompleteOnChildTransition

      public boolean shouldCompleteOnChildTransition(ActivityExecution scopeExecution)
      Invoked before a direct child execution leaves its activity via an outgoing transition. Returns true when the completion condition is already satisfied and remaining instances are to be canceled, so that the child execution ends instead of flowing onward — which completes the subprocess and cancels all remaining activities.
      Specified by:
      shouldCompleteOnChildTransition in interface AdHocCompositeActivityBehavior
      Parameters:
      scopeExecution - scope execution for the activity which defined the behavior
      Returns:
      true if the ad-hoc scope should complete instead of propagating the child execution along the transition

      protected boolean isAdHocScopeExecution(ActivityExecution execution)
